evals.prompt: Parent Business Process: Required Minimum Distribution Processing — end-to-end process for identifying participants approaching RMD eligibility, calculating required amounts, and initiating disbursements. Parent Sub-Process: RMD Calculation — calculates the required minimum distribution amount for each eligible participant. Ubiquitous Language document provided (defines: participant, RMD, plan balance, applicable divisor). Requirement to evaluate: 'The system will query the database to retrieve the participant's plan balance and divisor, then compute and store the RMD amount in the calculation table.'
evals.expected_output: Report flagging two violations: (1) Tag 1 — Describes HOW not WHAT — the phrases 'query the database', 'retrieve', 'compute and store', and 'calculation table' describe implementation mechanisms; (2) Tag 4 — Contains design details — 'database', 'calculation table' are data structure references belonging at the Capability/Feature level. Proposed rewrite removes all system internals and expresses the business outcome: something like 'Participant RMD amount must be calculated using the current plan balance and applicable divisor.' One-sentence diagnosis per violation naming the triggering phrase. No invented specificity.
assertions: Tag 1 (Describes HOW not WHAT) is identified
Tag 4 (Contains design details) is identified
Each violation has a one-sentence diagnosis naming a specific triggering phrase
Proposed rewrite contains no database, system, or technology references
Proposed rewrite uses 'participant' (UL doc term) not a synonym
Proposed rewrite uses a standard modal verb (must/shall/should/may/will)
No additional violations invented beyond what is actually present

evals.prompt: Parent Business Process: Required Minimum Distribution Processing. Parent Sub-Process: Participant Engagement — notifies participants of their RMD obligation and collects required elections. Ubiquitous Language document provided. Requirement to evaluate: 'As a participant, I want to receive my RMD notification so that I can make my disbursement election before the deadline.'
evals.expected_output: Report flagging Tag 3 (Uses Agile user-story format) before any rewrite is proposed. The diagnosis must explicitly name the 'As a participant, I want... so that...' structure as reserved for Capabilities and Features. Proposed rewrite converts to a BPR using an EARS-inspired pattern, e.g.: 'When a participant becomes RMD-eligible, RMD educational notification must be delivered via the participant's e-delivery preference before [deadline — to be confirmed with stakeholders].' Residual question about the specific deadline. Format violation flagged first — not silently skipped.
assertions: Tag 3 (Uses Agile user-story format) is flagged explicitly
Diagnosis names the 'As a participant, I want' structure as the triggering pattern
Diagnosis states that this format is reserved for Capabilities and Features, not BPRs
Proposed rewrite does NOT use the user-story format
Proposed rewrite uses a standard modal verb
If a deadline placeholder is needed, it appears as [placeholder] not an invented value
Rewrite is presented AFTER the violation diagnosis, not before

evals.prompt: Parent Business Process: Required Minimum Distribution Processing. Parent Sub-Process: Spousal Waiver Collection — collects and validates spousal consent waivers before disbursement proceeds. Ubiquitous Language document provided. Requirements to evaluate: (1) 'While a spousal waiver is pending, the RMD disbursement must not proceed to the disbursement sub-process.' (2) 'If the spousal waiver form is not received within 30 days, the participant must be notified by the VS Business Lead.'
evals.expected_output: Both requirements receive COMPLIANT status with no proposed rewrites. No phantom improvements, no style edits, no added words. Requirement 1 is a valid EARS State-driven pattern. Requirement 2 is a valid EARS Unwanted-behavior pattern. The summary line shows 2 requirements reviewed, 2 compliant.
assertions: Both requirements are marked COMPLIANT — no changes required
No violation tags are applied to either requirement
No rewrite is proposed for either requirement
Summary line shows 2 compliant, 0 violations
No phantom style improvements are suggested

evals.prompt: Parent Business Process: Required Minimum Distribution Processing. Parent Sub-Process: RMD Calculation — calculates the required minimum distribution amount. Ubiquitous Language document provided. Requirement to evaluate: 'When the participant submits a cancel-in-flight RMD request, the in-progress RMD transaction must be halted and funds returned to the participant account.'
evals.expected_output: Report flagging Tag 9 (Wrong parent) — the cancellation scenario describes an activity that is out of scope for the RMD Calculation sub-process, which handles calculation not cancellation. The diagnosis must name 'cancel-in-flight RMD request' and 'halted' as signals that this describes a cancellation flow that no current sub-process covers. Suggestion to consider running process-cohesion-decomposition (A4 Job 2) to check whether a cancellation sub-process needs to be added.
assertions: Tag 9 (Wrong parent) is flagged
Diagnosis names the 'cancel-in-flight' activity as out of scope for RMD Calculation
Output suggests the requirement may belong under a different or missing sub-process
Output references the possibility of running A4-Job2 to check process cohesion
No rewrite is proposed that forces the requirement to stay under RMD Calculation
